sources.name: Farama-Foundation/Gymnasium
sources.why: Maintained reference API and baseline classic-control/toy-text RL environments.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as the Python compatibility boundary; mirror selected low-dimensional tasks into Rust when hot loops matter.

sources.name: Farama-Foundation/Minigrid
sources.why: Fast configurable grid-world curriculum for partial observability, memory, planning, and instruction following.
sources.adapter_plan: Start with Empty, DoorKey, FourRooms, Memory, DynamicObstacles, and MultiRoom as seed-stable frontiers.

sources.name: google-deepmind/bsuite
sources.why: Diagnostic RL behavior suite for exploration, memory, credit assignment, and generalization.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as a benchmark ledger source before adding harder open-ended environments.

sources.name: MichaelTMatthews/Craftax
sources.why: Fast JAX open-ended benchmark with symbolic and pixel modes, useful as a long-horizon survival/crafting frontier.
sources.adapter_plan: Keep as optional JAX benchmark; use symbolic mode first, then pixel mode after resource profiling.

sources.name: minerllabs/minerl
sources.why: Historical Minecraft RL environment and dataset interface useful as a compatibility/reference lane when local licensing and license compatibility are explicit.
sources.adapter_plan: Keep metadata-only until license compatibility is resolved. Use local licensed Minecraft runtime only; never download game assets or credentials.

sources.name: MineDojo/MineDojo
sources.why: Open-ended Minecraft task/knowledge harness for long-horizon agents, crafting, navigation, survival, and player-instruction pressure.
sources.adapter_plan: Clone source for local adapter contracts; promote to full runtime only with local licensed Minecraft install, Java readiness, deterministic seeds, and disposable local worlds.

sources.name: microsoft/malmo
sources.why: Research-focused Minecraft mission/control API suitable for local loopback tasks, navigation, item collection, and scripted world contracts.
sources.adapter_plan: Clone source for local mission-contract adapters; use import/runtime probing until a local Minecraft/Malmo mission can run in a disposable world without public server access.

sources.name: MineDojo/Voyager
sources.why: Minecraft skill-library and lifelong-learning reference architecture. Theseus may learn from its task/skill schema, but cannot use external LLM inference as a solver.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata/reference only by default. Port local skill schema and evaluation ideas into Theseus loop closure without provider inference.

sources.name: openai/procgen
sources.why: Procedural visual-control benchmark designed to test sample efficiency and generalization.
sources.adapter_plan: Use source archive and metadata first; runtime integration may need build tooling on Windows.

sources.name: Farama-Foundation/PettingZoo
sources.why: Standard multi-agent RL API with cooperative and competitive reference tasks.
sources.adapter_plan: Use classic and MPE-style tasks first; avoid Atari/ROM-backed tasks unless rights are explicit.

sources.name: dvruette/pygba
sources.why: GBA-focused Gymnasium wrapper around mGBA. It is the most direct path from user-supplied GBA ROMs to local RL frontiers.
sources.adapter_plan: Use with user-owned local ROMs only. Start with its Pokemon Emerald wrapper, then add project-owned reward wrappers.

sources.name: mgba-emu/mgba
sources.why: Native emulator core used by PyGBA for user-supplied GBA ROM reinforcement learning.
sources.adapter_plan: Stage source and build Python CFFI bindings in the isolated emulator runtime. Treat Windows CFFI/header generation failures as a runtime gate, not teacher architecture work.

sources.name: Baekalfen/PyBoy
sources.why: Python Game Boy/Game Boy Color emulator with APIs commonly used for scripting and Gymnasium-style RL wrappers.
sources.adapter_plan: Use for user-owned GB/GBC ROMs after GPL compatibility and package/runtime smoke are explicit.

sources.name: gymboy
sources.why: Gymnasium package for Game Boy/Game Boy Color environments, including Tetris, Pokemon, Kirby, and Super Mario Land variants.
sources.adapter_plan: Use only with user-owned local ROMs. Prefer minimal-image/flatten modes first for efficient local training.

sources.name: Farama-Foundation/stable-retro
sources.why: Maintained fork of Gym Retro for emulator-backed RL with game integration metadata and local ROM import workflows.
sources.adapter_plan: Use metadata first because the source archive is large; stage code only after checksum/import integration is explicit. Use for user-owned ROMs only; no autonomous ROM downloads.

sources.name: jjshoots/PyFlyt
sources.why: MIT-licensed UAV simulator with Gymnasium and PettingZoo environments; best first drone RL smoke target before high-fidelity race simulation.
sources.adapter_plan: Start with PyFlyt/QuadX-Hover-v2 and fixed random/PID-like policies, then add gate-following and waypoint rewards.

sources.name: learnsyslab/gym-pybullet-drones
sources.why: Gymnasium-compatible PyBullet quadrotor control environments with hover and multi-agent examples.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as the lightweight local quadrotor dynamics lane; keep SITL and real firmware examples disabled until explicitly requested.

sources.name: microsoft/AirSim-Drone-Racing-Lab
sources.why: Race-specific AirSim framework with Python API, baselines, and Windows/Linux binaries for drone racing research.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as a race-like simulator lane after basic hover/waypoint control works; require simulator process health before runtime smoke.

sources.name: ntnu-arl/aerial_gym_simulator
sources.why: Isaac Gym-based high-throughput aerial robot simulator for massively parallel MAV RL once NVIDIA stack support is ready.
sources.adapter_plan: Clone source for adapter and contract inspection. Promote execution only after Isaac/Aerial Gym runtime and GPU budget smoke pass on a suitable machine.

sources.name: microsoft/AirSim
sources.why: Open-source Unreal/Unity simulator for drones and vehicles with APIs for imagery, state, and control; useful as legacy reference for race simulation.
sources.adapter_plan: Clone source for legacy API compatibility and adapters because the upstream project is archived and Unreal setup is heavy. Prefer AirSim Drone Racing Lab for race work.

sources.name: mavlink/MAVSDK-Python
sources.why: Python MAVSDK client matching the AI Grand Prix-style MAVLink/MAVSDK-compatible SITL bridge.
sources.adapter_plan: Use only against simulator/SITL endpoints by default; live vehicle endpoints require explicit human approval and failsafe proof.

sources.name: PX4/PX4-Autopilot
sources.why: PX4 SITL is the standard bridge for testing MAVSDK/PX4 control logic before any hardware-facing work.
sources.adapter_plan: Clone source for SITL contract metadata. Prefer prebuilt/container SITL packages and never connect to real flight hardware without explicit operator approval.

sources.name: web-arena-x/webarena
sources.why: Self-hosted web-agent benchmark for realistic browser tasks across shopping, forum, GitLab, map, and content environments.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata first. Build local browser/action adapter only after sandbox, self-hosted services, and no-live-account side-effect gates are explicit.

sources.name: xlang-ai/OSWorld
sources.why: Real operating-system benchmark for multimodal computer-use agents with execution-based task grading.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata first. Use only after VM sandbox, grader isolation, and safety/reflex gates are ready.

sources.name: ServiceNow/BrowserGym
sources.why: Gym-style framework that unifies web-agent benchmark environments, including WebArena-style tasks.
sources.adapter_plan: Stage source as the local browser-task adapter layer. Full browser runs require Playwright/browser isolation, no-live-account policy, and local Theseus endpoint routing.

sources.name: sierra-research/tau-bench
sources.why: Tool-Agent-User benchmark for multi-turn policy-constrained tool use in realistic domains.
sources.adapter_plan: Use metadata first; add local tool API simulator and policy verifier before benchmark pressure.

sources.name: sierra-research/tau2-bench
sources.why: Current tau-bench successor with tau three text, knowledge, voice, task-fix, and gym interfaces for long-horizon tool-agent-user evaluation.
sources.adapter_plan: Use text/gym domains first through a local tool simulator and local Theseus endpoint. Voice/provider paths and external LLM user simulators stay disabled unless separately approved.

sources.name: ShishirPatil/gorilla:berkeley-function-call-leaderboard
sources.why: Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard pressures function/tool-call selection, AST-style call validity, multi-turn tool use, relevance detection, and format sensitivity.
sources.adapter_plan: Stage Gorilla source and treat BFCL as public calibration only. Use local endpoint generation/evaluation paths, disable external provider calls, and keep web-search/API categories gated unless local substitutes exist.

sources.name: apple/ToolSandbox
sources.why: Stateful conversational tool-use benchmark with implicit state dependencies, user simulation, intermediate milestones, canonicalization, and insufficient-information stress cases.
sources.adapter_plan: Keep metadata-only until license terms are manually accepted for this project. If approved, use as a stateful local tool-use calibration lane only, never as training data.

sources.name: Farama-Foundation/Metaworld
sources.why: Meta- and multi-task manipulation benchmark with 50 Sawyer robot tasks.
sources.adapter_plan: Queue as robotics frontier; only run after MuJoCo and Windows support are verified.

sources.name: instadeepai/jumanji
sources.why: JAX-based industry-grade RL environment suite for combinatorial optimization, routing, packing, and logic tasks.
sources.adapter_plan: Stage source for adapter design; run only after JAX install/resource smoke passes, then start with small deterministic graph/logic tasks.

sources.name: google/brax
sources.why: Fast differentiable physics and RL environments used for accelerated control and JAX training experiments.
sources.adapter_plan: Keep as optional JAX/physics frontier; smoke import and a tiny ant/hopper-style reset before any training use.

sources.name: google-deepmind/dm_control
sources.why: DeepMind Control Suite is a common continuous-control benchmark family for locomotion and manipulation.
sources.adapter_plan: Stage source only; run after MuJoCo/runtime compatibility is verified on the target worker.

sources.name: sail-sg/envpool
sources.why: High-throughput batched RL environment runtime useful for future parallel rollout acceleration.
sources.adapter_plan: Stage source for reference; only compile after Windows/native toolchain gate passes or on Linux/mac worker.

sources.name: danijar/crafter
sources.why: Compact open-ended survival benchmark with procedural world dynamics and long-horizon reward structure.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as a mid-stage symbolic/pixel RL frontier after Gymnasium/Minigrid and before heavier emulator tasks.

sources.name: kenjyoung/MinAtar
sources.why: Small arcade-like RL environments that pressure perception and control without commercial ROMs.
sources.adapter_plan: Keep metadata-only until GPL compatibility is accepted for this lane.

sources.name: Farama-Foundation/Arcade-Learning-Environment
sources.why: Canonical Atari RL interface, useful for planning user-owned ROM integration and public benchmark comparability.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata only. Never fetch Atari ROMs autonomously; require user-owned ROM inventory and GPL/runtime policy review.

sources.name: facebookresearch/nle
sources.why: NetHack Learning Environment is a hard long-horizon symbolic RL benchmark for exploration, planning, and inventory management.
sources.adapter_plan: Queue until license/runtime status is audited; prefer metadata and benchmark card only.

sources.name: vwxyzjn/cleanrl
sources.why: Widely used single-file RL implementation reference for PPO/DQN/SAC patterns and reproducible training scripts.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata/reference only until license is confirmed; do not import as training code automatically.

sources.name: EleutherAI/lm-evaluation-harness
sources.why: Widely used local evaluation harness with many language, reasoning, coding, and long-context tasks.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as public calibration only until SymLiquid exposes a compatible LM scoring interface.

sources.name: openai/simple-evals
sources.why: Compact public eval harness used around frontier model releases for GPQA, MMLU-style, math, coding, and instruction-following calibration.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as a small public calibration harness after SymLiquid exposes compatible scoring/chat adapters; never call external models from this harness.

sources.name: stanford-crfm/helm
sources.why: Holistic multi-metric language-model evaluation framework for calibration, robustness, fairness, and scenario coverage.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as benchmark metadata and public calibration first; stage source only after task IO contracts are explicit.

sources.name: google/BIG-bench
sources.why: Broad benchmark/task collection useful for discovering new pressure surfaces and bridge tasks.
sources.adapter_plan: Use metadata first, then queue tasks by IO shape; prefer small deterministic subsets as diagnostics before public calibration.

sources.name: openai/human-eval
sources.why: Small Python code-generation benchmark with unit-test style execution, useful as an early coding pressure surface.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata first. Add sandboxed local code execution adapter before any score claim.

sources.name: bigcode-project/bigcodebench
sources.why: Function-level code-generation benchmark with more complex instructions and library usage than HumanEval/MBPP.
sources.adapter_plan: Use after sandboxed Python execution and dependency isolation are working; start with metadata and one safe task smoke.

sources.name: LiveCodeBench/LiveCodeBench
sources.why: Contamination-aware coding benchmark with newer problems and code execution/self-repair task families.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata/source first; run only inside sandboxed generated-code execution lane with network off.

sources.name: google-research/google-research
sources.why: Google Research repository containing MBPP-style coding benchmark assets; useful after HumanEval adapter smoke.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata first because the repository is broad; stage only MBPP-relevant assets after adapter plan is explicit.

sources.name: hendrycks/test
sources.why: Original MMLU/MATH benchmark source repository, useful for public calibration and regression context.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as public calibration metadata/source; prefer private mutated/internal analogs for frontier pressure.

sources.name: TIGER-Lab/MMLU-Pro
sources.why: Harder MMLU-style public calibration surface with broader option sets and reasoning pressure.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata/tiny samples only until leakage and scoring adapters are ready.

sources.name: Idavidrein/gpqa
sources.why: Graduate-level science QA benchmark frequently used in frontier model public evaluations.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata/tiny samples only; use as calibration after answer-choice scoring and contamination checks.

sources.name: gaia-benchmark/GAIA
sources.why: Public general-assistant benchmark for tool use, browsing, multimodal reasoning, and multi-step task execution.
sources.adapter_plan: Queue until license/access terms and local no-external-inference harness are explicit.

sources.name: Aider-AI/polyglot-benchmark
sources.why: Multi-language coding benchmark used by an agentic coding tool community; useful later for coding-agent calibration.
sources.adapter_plan: Queue until license is confirmed; prefer metadata and compatibility notes before any clone or execution.

sources.name: SWE-bench/SWE-bench
sources.why: Repository-level software engineering benchmark for issue-to-patch behavior; later-stage coding agent pressure.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata first. Use only after sandboxed repo checkout, patch isolation, and regression test execution are automated locally.

sources.name: GeniusHTX/SWE-Skills-Bench
sources.why: Requirement-driven software-engineering benchmark that measures marginal value of agent skills, useful for Theseus Hive skill lifecycle and tool-compiler validation.
sources.adapter_plan: Stage source and build a local-endpoint, no-provider smoke. The upstream Claude/API-key runner is forbidden during scoring until replaced by a local Theseus adapter.

sources.name: QuantaAlpha/GitTaskBench
sources.why: Repo-level real-world code-agent benchmark spanning repository understanding, environment setup, incremental development, bug fixing, multimodal file outputs, and task delivery.
sources.adapter_plan: Queue until license terms are explicit. If cleared, use only as public calibration with local endpoint, sandboxed execution, and no bundled ground-truth leakage into training.

sources.name: SWE-agent/mini-swe-agent
sources.why: Compact SWE-bench-style agent harness useful for later local coding-agent scaffolding and regression tests.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as harness source only; route generated code through sandbox and local model adapters, not external model APIs.

sources.name: evalplus/evalplus
sources.why: HumanEval+/MBPP+ add stronger generated-code test suites and efficiency-oriented pressure beyond the original small coding benchmarks.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as a sandboxed local code-eval harness only. Provider backends and external model calls are forbidden during Theseus scoring.

sources.name: anomalyco/opencode
sources.why: OpenCode is an actively used terminal coding-agent harness with OpenAI-compatible endpoint support, useful for training Theseus to operate inside real coding workflows.
sources.adapter_plan: Use the source as a local harness integration target. All provider/model paths stay disabled during scoring; the only solver endpoint is the local Theseus/OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

sources.name: anomalyco/opencode-bench
sources.why: OpenCode-style benchmark candidate for real repository coding-agent pressure.
sources.adapter_plan: Keep metadata-only until the repository license is explicit or the user approves a rights override. If approved, use only with local Theseus endpoint and provider judges disabled.

sources.name: SWE-agent/SWE-agent
sources.why: Full SWE-agent harness gives Theseus a stronger issue-to-patch workflow reference than mini-swe-agent once sandboxing is ready.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as a local harness/reference. Disable external model providers and route only through Theseus/local endpoint contracts during benchmark scoring.

sources.name: OpenHands/OpenHands
sources.why: OpenHands provides a broad software-agent sandbox architecture and SWE-bench-oriented workflow references.
sources.adapter_plan: Clone source for local architecture and task-contract reference. Do not run provider-backed agents; full harness execution requires local sandbox/runtime gates.

sources.name: harbor-framework/terminal-bench
sources.why: Terminal-Bench pressures real CLI, build, repair, data, and systems tasks with task-specific verification scripts.
sources.adapter_plan: Use only through local terminal sandboxes and Theseus endpoint contracts. Full execution needs a container runtime; metadata/task-contract pressure can run without provider inference.

sources.name: TheAgentCompany/TheAgentCompany
sources.why: Simulated software-company benchmark combining browsing, coding, terminal use, collaboration tools, files, and coworker communication.
sources.adapter_plan: Stage source only. Do not run the heavy service/data setup autonomously; full runtime needs Docker, 30+ GB disk, account isolation, local endpoint routing, and no public gateway exposure.

sources.name: CodeClash-ai/CodeClash
sources.why: Goal-oriented software-engineering tournament benchmark that adds competitive, multi-round pressure beyond one-shot bug fixing.
sources.adapter_plan: Stage as an arena/score-contract source. Route competitors to local Theseus checkpoints only; external model APIs and public tournament submission are forbidden by default.

sources.name: scaleapi/SWE-Atlas
sources.why: SWE-Atlas covers codebase Q&A, test writing, and refactoring, giving Theseus coding pressure beyond issue resolution.
sources.adapter_plan: Use repository exploration and artifact scoring locally. Hosted/LLM rubric graders are forbidden during scoring until a local rubric scorer exists.

sources.name: amazon-science/SWE-PolyBench
sources.why: Multilingual repository-level benchmark for Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, and Java-style coding agent transfer.
sources.adapter_plan: Stage as a polyglot repo-level pressure lane after sandboxed build/test execution is ready.

sources.name: SWE-agent/SWE-ReX
sources.why: Sandboxed execution infrastructure used by SWE-agent; useful for Theseus local code execution and patch-eval isolation.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as a reference/runtime lane for local sandbox execution. Prefer local Docker or Podman; cloud backends stay disabled unless explicitly approved.

sources.name: SWE-bench/SWE-smith
sources.why: SWE-smith is a task-generation and training-data pipeline for software-engineering agents, useful for self-generated coding curricula.
sources.adapter_plan: Use only to generate governed local tasks from permitted repos. Docker/Ubuntu assumptions are runtime-gated; generated tasks must pass license, leakage, and verifier checks.

sources.name: abundant-ai/SWE-gen
sources.why: SWE-gen-style PR-to-task tooling can help Theseus synthesize fresh repository repair environments from permitted local repos.
sources.adapter_plan: Keep as a task-generation reference. Any generated benchmark must keep source provenance, base commit, tests, license, and contamination metadata.

sources.name: Leolty/repobench
sources.why: Repository-level code completion benchmark for multi-file context and retrieval pressure.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata-only until attribution requirements and task loader are explicit; useful for context/memory transfer evals.

sources.name: DeepSoftwareAnalytics/RepoTransBench
sources.why: Repository-level code translation benchmark for cross-language transfer through arms.
sources.adapter_plan: Keep queue-only until license is explicit, then use as a multilingual transfer diagnostic.

sources.name: openai/SWELancer-Benchmark
sources.why: Freelance-style software-engineering benchmark reference for later real-world coding task pressure.
sources.adapter_plan: Archived and license-uncertain; keep metadata-only and prefer OpenAI preparedness references only as public calibration metadata.

sources.name: UKGovernmentBEIS/inspect_evals
sources.why: Public evaluation implementations for many model capabilities, useful as source/reference for adapter and scorer design.
sources.adapter_plan: Clone source as a reference harness; run only local/offline tasks after scorer contracts and external-inference audit pass.

sources.name: babylm/babylm-evaluation-pipeline
sources.why: Official BabyLM 2026 evaluation pipeline and leaderboard comparator.
sources.adapter_plan: Queue because the local project already has a BabyLM eval clone; verify license/commit before importing another copy.

sources.name: HuggingFaceFW/fineweb-edu
sources.why: Large educational web corpus with published filtering methodology and sample configs.
sources.adapter_plan: Do not bulk download. Add a small streamed sample only after dedupe, toxicity, license, and domain-balance gates.
sources.sample_plan: stream <=1000 docs from sample-10BT into ignored candidate data, then score with local quality filters

sampling.purpose: tiny governed educational web sample for low-ratio local pairwise distillation only

sources.name: HuggingFaceFW/fineweb
sources.why: Large open web corpus with documented curation, useful as a broad data source reference after quality and domain gates.
sources.adapter_plan: Do not bulk download. Use metadata and tiny controlled samples only when the data sampler requests general-web contrast data.
sources.sample_plan: stream <=48 rows only after dedupe, PII, quality, and source-mix gates pass

sampling.reason: fineweb_edu is preferred for current small-model educational pressure; keep broad web metadata-only

sources.name: HuggingFaceFW/fineweb-2
sources.why: Newer multilingual FineWeb-family corpus candidate for future multilingual and broad-coverage lanes.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata only until multilingual benchmarks and tokenizer/data-mix policies are explicit.
sources.sample_plan: none until language coverage, leakage, and storage budgets are approved

sampling.reason: future multilingual lane; avoid broad sampling during current local benchmark setup

sources.name: HuggingFaceTB/smollm-corpus
sources.why: Smaller deduplicated corpus family aligned with small-model training constraints.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as a candidate for small controlled text samples, not as an automatic bulk source.
sources.sample_plan: stream a tiny educational subset after source mix and overlap checks

sampling.purpose: tiny governed deduplicated educational web sample for low-ratio local pairwise distillation only

sources.name: Open-Orca/OpenOrca
sources.why: Broad MIT licensed instruction-response corpus for extraction, QA, summarization, reasoning, and response-shape pressure.
sources.adapter_plan: Use tiny governed D: samples only; never treat as benchmark evidence; keep eval-overlap filters active.
sources.sample_plan: stream <=24 rows into the governed D: sample pantry for low-ratio local pairwise and instruction-pressure experiments

sampling.purpose: tiny governed instruction-response sample for low-ratio local augmentation only

sources.name: AI-MO/NuminaMath-CoT
sources.why: Apache-2.0 math problem-solution traces for symbolic reasoning, final-answer discipline, and step repair.
sources.adapter_plan: Use tiny governed D: samples and keep math benchmarks eval-only; preserve source tags and no public benchmark promotion claims.
sources.sample_plan: stream <=24 rows into the governed D: sample pantry for low-ratio local reasoning pressure

sampling.purpose: tiny governed math reasoning sample for low-ratio local augmentation only

sources.name: NousResearch/hermes-function-calling-v1
sources.why: Apache-2.0 function-calling conversations for schema adherence, tool selection, abstention, and argument planning.
sources.adapter_plan: Use tiny governed D: samples for private tool-use pressure; score BFCL and other public tool benchmarks only as calibration.
sources.sample_plan: stream <=24 rows from func_calling_singleturn after license and overlap gates

sampling.purpose: tiny governed tool-use sample for local interface and schema pressure only

sources.name: argilla/Synth-APIGen-v0.1
sources.why: Apache-2.0 synthetic APIGen rows with tool schemas, user queries, and answer calls for interface-grounded tool-use pressure.
sources.adapter_plan: Use tiny governed D: samples only; avoid Salesforce xLAM gated source for automatic ingest.
sources.sample_plan: stream <=24 rows into the governed D: sample pantry for low-ratio tool-schema pressure

sampling.purpose: tiny governed synthetic function-calling sample for local tool-use augmentation only

sources.name: OpenAssistant/oasst2
sources.why: Apache-2.0 human-feedback conversation tree rows useful after parent/child context reconstruction.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata only until a tree assembler and language/review filters are promoted.
sources.sample_plan: none until parent/child context assembly preserves conversation turns

sampling.reason: requires tree reconstruction before useful training rows

sources.name: allenai/dolma
sources.why: Transparent multi-source pretraining corpus with documented versions and samples.
sources.adapter_plan: Use metadata and data-sheet first; only sample small subsets after PII and overlap gates.
sources.sample_plan: prefer v1_6-sample metadata; avoid bulk download

sampling.reason: dataset viewer rows unavailable in this environment; keep metadata-only until a bounded sample endpoint is verified

sources.name: HuggingFaceTB/cosmopedia
sources.why: Open synthetic textbook/blog/story corpus useful for studying compact educational data mixtures after provenance and quality checks.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata only by default. Sample tiny slices only after dedupe, source-quality, and synthetic-ratio gates pass.
sources.sample_plan: stream <=48 rows as a low-ratio candidate only after dataset viewer config is verified

sampling.reason: keep metadata-only until local quality filters are tuned for synthetic educational data

sources.name: open-web-math/open-web-math
sources.why: Math-heavy corpus candidate for future mathematical reasoning growth lanes.
sources.adapter_plan: Queue for license/data-sheet review before any sampling or training use.
sources.sample_plan: none until license and overlap policy are explicit

sampling.reason: license and contamination audit required first

sources.name: Salesforce/wikitext
sources.why: Common language-modeling benchmark/data reference, useful for public comparability but not currently in the allowed data-license set.
sources.adapter_plan: Queue for license compatibility review; do not train on it automatically.
sources.sample_plan: none until share-alike/GFDL obligations are reviewed

sampling.reason: license obligations require explicit review before sampling or training use

sources.name: codeparrot/apps
sources.why: APPS provides competitive-programming-style prompts, solutions, and tests for coding skill growth.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata first. Tiny samples only after leakage checks against HumanEval/MBPP/EvalPlus/LiveCodeBench and sandboxed execution policy.
sources.sample_plan: tiny governed task/test metadata only; no bulk download

sampling.reason: enable only after code-sandbox leakage filters are refreshed

sources.name: deepmind/code_contests
sources.why: DeepMind CodeContests adds harder algorithmic programming pressure with tests and multiple solution traces.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as attribution-preserving metadata and later tiny governed shards; run only inside local sandbox and never mix eval splits into training.
sources.sample_plan: tiny governed examples after attribution and contamination checks

sampling.reason: requires attribution tracking and code-eval leakage gates before sampling

sources.name: bigcode/the-stack-v2
sources.why: The Stack v2 is a broad code corpus reference for future local code pretraining mixtures.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata only until per-file license filters, opt-out handling, PII/secrets scanning, and storage budget are implemented.
sources.sample_plan: none by default; only license-filtered tiny shards after governance expands

sampling.reason: gated dataset with per-file license obligations; no automatic sampling

sources.name: bigcode/starcoderdata
sources.why: StarCoderData is a reference code pretraining corpus with governance lessons for license filtering and opt-out handling.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata only; use to shape policy and future license-filtered code data recipes rather than bulk training now.
sources.sample_plan: none until per-file license and opt-out filters are implemented

sampling.reason: requires BigCode-style governance and license-filtering before use

sources.name: code-search-net/code_search_net
sources.why: CodeSearchNet is useful for code-text retrieval, docstring alignment, and code understanding transfer.
sources.adapter_plan: Metadata only until license obligations and train/eval contamination checks are explicit.
sources.sample_plan: none until license and split policy are reviewed

sources.name: NVIDIA/RULER
sources.why: RULER is a long-context benchmark generator covering retrieval, multi-hop tracking, aggregation, and robustness beyond simple needle recall.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as public calibration metadata only. Map task categories into private VCM context-recovery analogues without copying public prompts, contexts, answers, traces, or templates.
smoke_plan: run private VCM context-recovery smoke with local fixtures only
sources.sample_plan: no public examples or generated benchmark rows enter training

sources.name: BABILong
sources.why: BABILong stresses fact tracking and reasoning over long irrelevant background text, which maps directly to VCM evidence selection and distraction rejection.
sources.adapter_plan: Keep benchmark content calibration-only; create private fact-in-noise fixtures for training and regression pressure.
smoke_plan: verify no BABILong rows are admitted to training
sources.sample_plan: metadata only; no public rows are used as training samples

sources.name: NeedleBench / OpenCompass
sources.why: NeedleBench covers single retrieval, multi retrieval, multi reasoning, and atomic-to-composite long-context evaluation families.
sources.adapter_plan: Use OpenCompass/NeedleBench only as public calibration metadata; mirror categories with private VCM fixtures.
sources.sample_plan: metadata only; no public needles, haystacks, or answer templates enter training

sources.name: snap-research/LoCoMo
sources.why: LoCoMo targets long-term conversational memory, temporal/event summaries, and personalized QA.
sources.adapter_plan: Queue only because the license is non-commercial. Use its task taxonomy for human review, not active import or training.
smoke_plan: block active benchmark card until license policy changes
do not stage rows or examples
sources.sample_plan: none; license is not allowed for active import

sources.name: LongMemEval
sources.why: LongMemEval covers extraction, multi-session reasoning, updates, temporal reasoning, and abstention over long memory histories.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as public calibration metadata and derive private event-history fixtures with explicit stale/update handling.
sources.sample_plan: metadata only; no public memory questions or answers enter training

sources.name: LongMemEval-V2
sources.why: LongMemEval-V2 expands long-memory QA with large trajectory histories and explicit memory ability labels.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as public calibration metadata and map abilities into private VCM stale/update/abstention regression fixtures.
smoke_plan: verify no public rows are admitted to training
sources.sample_plan: metadata only; no public trajectories enter training

sources.name: Princeton NLP HELMET
sources.why: HELMET is a long-context benchmark harness spanning retrieval, reasoning, generation, and summarization categories across controlled lengths.
sources.adapter_plan: Use harness taxonomy as public calibration metadata; do not use public items for training pressure.
smoke_plan: link to private VCM context-recovery smoke
sources.sample_plan: metadata only; no public tasks enter training

sources.name: THUDM/LongBench v2
sources.why: LongBench v2 focuses on long-context deep understanding and reasoning across very long context lengths.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as public calibration metadata and keep any future calibration operator-locked.
smoke_plan: verify no public rows enter training
sources.sample_plan: metadata only; public prompts and answer labels stay out of training

sources.name: OpenBMB/InfiniteBench
sources.why: InfiniteBench targets 100k-plus context evaluation across retrieval, QA, summarization, and code/debugging long-context tasks.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as calibration metadata; create private context-scale stressors from Theseus artifacts instead of importing public items into training.
sources.sample_plan: metadata only; no public long-context items enter training

sources.name: adobe-research/NoLiMa
sources.why: NoLiMa stresses minimal lexical overlap between questions and evidence, directly pressuring VCM alias/cue bridge recovery.
sources.adapter_plan: Block public payload staging under the current non-commercial license policy; use private lexical-disconnect analogues only.
sources.sample_plan: none; public data is not imported under current license policy

sources.name: Michelangelo / Latent Structure Queries
sources.why: Michelangelo/LSQ tests latent structure, MRCR, latent list, and IDK behavior beyond needle retrieval.
sources.adapter_plan: Verify official source and license before public scoring; mirror latent-structure categories with private analogues.
sources.sample_plan: none until official source/license is verified

sources.name: infinigence/LVEval
sources.why: LV-Eval spans 16k to 256k context QA with confusing facts and keyword/phrase replacement.
sources.adapter_plan: Use metadata until license partition and deterministic scoring are reviewed; mirror confusing-fact length buckets privately.
sources.sample_plan: metadata only until dataset partition review

sources.name: LOFT / Long-Context Frontiers
sources.why: LOFT covers retrieval, RAG, SQL-like structured queries, and many-shot long-context task substitution up to million-token contexts.
sources.adapter_plan: Locate official payloads/license and admit only deterministic text/RAG/SQL/many-shot lanes; use private structured-memory analogues first.
smoke_plan: verify license and local deterministic scorers
sources.sample_plan: none until official payloads and license are verified

sources.name: ibm/mt-rag-benchmark
sources.why: MTRAG targets multi-turn RAG memory with later-turn, non-standalone, unanswerable, and multi-domain pressure.
sources.adapter_plan: Verify license/evaluator determinism before public payload staging; use private multi-turn RAG analogues first.
sources.sample_plan: none until license and deterministic scoring are verified

sources.name: IBM/mt-rag-benchmark MTRAG-UN
sources.why: MTRAG-UN stresses unanswerable, underspecified, non-standalone, and unclear multi-turn RAG behavior.
sources.adapter_plan: Verify license/evaluator determinism before public payload staging; use private answerability analogues first.
sources.sample_plan: none until license and deterministic scoring are verified

sources.name: FACTS Grounding
sources.why: FACTS Grounding tests document-grounded long-form answer behavior, but its public/private leaderboard and model-judge path need separate admission.
sources.adapter_plan: Block public scoring until terms and local deterministic proxy are reviewed; use private grounded-span analogues only.
sources.sample_plan: none until terms and deterministic proxy are verified

sources.name: xjtuleeyf/Locomo-Plus
sources.why: LoCoMo-Plus targets cognitive memory and cue-trigger constraint disconnect, matching VCM's long-term user constraint lane.
sources.adapter_plan: Verify license and deterministic constraint-consistency metrics before public payload staging; use private cognitive-memory analogues first.
sources.sample_plan: none until license and metric are verified

sources.name: speechbrain/benchmarks
sources.why: Open benchmark harnesses for speech tasks that can pressure the native Theseus voice I/O lane.
sources.adapter_plan: Start with metadata and harness inspection. Use harnesses only as benchmark/scoring pressure; do not use pretrained SpeechBrain inference as a capability.
sources.sample_plan: metadata only until local voice model and data policy are ready

sources.name: mozilla-foundation/common_voice_17_0
sources.why: Large multilingual CC0 voice dataset candidate for Theseus-native STT and voice-router growth.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as license-gated native voice pressure. Keep locale/storage selection explicit and do not use pretrained speech inference.
sources.sample_plan: metadata first; tiny locale shards only through configs/native_voice_training_policy.json caps

sources.name: openslr/librispeech_asr
sources.why: Standard CC BY 4.0 ASR corpus for native STT bootstrap, evaluation, and voice packet pressure.
sources.adapter_plan: Use validation-clean as the tiny automatic bootstrap shard; larger train-clean archives require explicit disk/runtime approval.
sources.sample_plan: tiny governed audio/transcript rows through scripts/native_voice_training_manifest.py

sources.name: OpenSLR SLR60 LibriTTS
sources.why: CC BY 4.0 multi-speaker English corpus designed for TTS research.
sources.adapter_plan: Keep metadata-only until a shard downloader chooses specific OpenSLR archives and disk caps.
sources.sample_plan: planned TTS shards through native voice training policy; no bulk download by default

sources.name: keithito/lj_speech
sources.why: Public-domain single-speaker TTS dataset for native speech synthesis pressure.
sources.adapter_plan: Use as the first clean native TTS learner shard after the tiny shard fetcher is approved.
sources.sample_plan: metadata first; tiny governed TTS shard through native voice training policy

sources.name: CSTR VCTK Corpus
sources.why: Speaker-diverse English speech corpus useful for later native TTS speaker-conditioning pressure.
sources.adapter_plan: Queue until source-card attribution and storage requirements are reviewed directly.
sources.sample_plan: none until VCTK attribution/storage review is complete
